evals.prompt: Build a quoting function for ICHRA. It takes a zip code, FIP code, state, household members (each with age, tobacco/smoker status, and relationship), and a monthly HRA contribution amount. Call the HealthSherpa API and return plans grouped by enrollment path (api vs deeplink vs not enrollable). Display deductible and max out-of-pocket for each plan.
evals.expected_output: A function that POSTs to the correct base URL + /api/v1/quotes with the correct structure, groups results by enrollment flags, displays cost sharing correctly, and includes HRA context.
assertions: Uses the correct base URL (https://api.ichra.healthsherpa.com or https://api.ichra-staging.healthsherpa.com)
Uses POST method to /api/v1/quotes, not GET
Sends x-api-key header and Content-Type: application/json
Uses fip_code (not fips_code) in the quoting request
Uses applicants array (not household) with age (integer), smoker (boolean), and relationship fields
Includes off_ex: true in the quoting request body — the API only supports off-exchange enrollment and without this flag returns on-exchange plans which are not supported
Includes hra object with offered_hra: true, type: 'ichra', amount, contribution_covers, and start
Reads plans from response.plans array (response is {plans: [...], meta: {...}})
Groups or filters plans using both api_enrollment AND deeplink_enrollment flags — not just one
Handles the case where both flags are false (not enrollable)
Does not hardcode plan IDs or carrier names
Uses cost_sharing.medical_ded_ind for deductible display — NOT deductible_individual or ded_individual
Uses cost_sharing.medical_moop_ind for max OOP display — NOT moop_individual or max_out_of_pocket

evals.prompt: Write the code to create a complete enrollment application for a 30-year-old female named Jane Smith in Phoenix, AZ (85001, FIPS 04013). She has a $500/month ICHRA from employer 'Acme Corp' (FEIN 123456789). Plan HIOS is 53901AZ1490005, plan year 2026. The SEP reason is offered_ichra with event date 2026-03-01. Her agent is Bob Williams (NPN 98765432). Follow the full enrollment workflow from the skill, including fetching carrier-specific attestation content before building the payload. Include everything needed so this application can be immediately submitted.
evals.expected_output: A POST to the correct base URL + /api/v1/applications with a complete payload that would pass validation for any carrier.
assertions: Uses the correct base URL (https://api.ichra.healthsherpa.com or https://api.ichra-staging.healthsherpa.com)
POSTs to /api/v1/applications with x-api-key header and Content-Type: application/json
Includes applicants.primary.signature with the applicant's typed name — NOT under the signatures object
Includes signatures.signature_date as a separate field — NOT under applicants
Includes ssn (or itin) on the primary applicant
Includes us_citizen, resides_in_state, and uses_tobacco booleans on primary
Includes race_ethnicity and hispanic_origin on primary (can be 'decline_to_answer')
Includes language_spoken and language_written on primary
Uses street_address_1 for address fields, not street_line_1 or address_line_1
Uses gender (not sex) and uses_tobacco (not tobacco_use)
Employer address is nested under hra.employer.address using the standard address schema (street_address_1, city, state, zip_code)
Includes hra object with offered_hra, type, amount, contribution_covers, and start fields
Includes hra.employer with name, fein, and address — all three are needed for employer group association
Includes agent_of_record with at minimum first_name, last_name, and national_producer_number
Includes attestations with electronic_signature_consent: true and agrees_issuer_attestations: true
Includes special_enrollment_period with event_type and event_date
Does NOT set desired_effective_date (should omit it or explain why it's risky)
Fetches enrollment_requirements via GET /plans/:hios_id?include=enrollment_requirements BEFORE presenting attestations
Uses the carrier-specific attestation text from enrollment_requirements as the actual consent labels — does NOT use generic labels like 'I agree to issuer attestations'
Only renders attestation fields that are present in the enrollment_requirements response

evals.prompt: After creating an application, the response includes an errors array with a 'supporting_documentation_required' entry. Write the code to upload a PDF file as SEP documentation and then submit the application. Use the application_id from the create response.
evals.expected_output: Code that uploads a document with the correct document_type, then submits the application.
assertions: POSTs to /api/v1/applications/:id/supporting_documentation
Includes document_type: 'sep' as a top-level field alongside the file — not nested inside the file object
For JSON upload: structures the file as {filename, content_type, content_base64} — not raw binary
For multipart upload: sends file as binary with document_type as a separate form field
Uploads the document BEFORE calling submit, not after
Submits via POST to /api/v1/applications/:id/submit after upload succeeds
Expects 202 Accepted from submit (not 200)

evals.prompt: After submitting an application, I need to handle payment. Write code that reads the payment_instructions from the application response, determines the correct payment flow, and if payment redirect is supported, calls the payment redirect endpoint and builds an HTML form to send the user to the carrier's payment page.
evals.expected_output: Code that implements the payment decision tree and builds a form POST from the payment redirect response.
assertions: Reads payment_instructions from the application response — does not hardcode payment behavior
Checks payment_required_with_submission first (pay before submit)
Checks payment_redirect_supported second (pay after submit)
Checks pay_by_phone_supported as fallback (show phone number)
Calls GET /api/v1/applications/:id/payment_redirect — uses GET, not POST
Builds an HTML form with method and action from the response endpoint
Includes all fields from the response fields array as hidden inputs
Auto-submits the form or provides a mechanism to redirect the user's browser
Does NOT try to parse or interpret the field names/values — passes them through exactly

evals.prompt: I need to update an existing application to add a spouse dependent and change the primary's email. The application ID is HSA000738818 and the primary applicant's member_id is 12345. The application already has the primary applicant and all required fields populated.
evals.expected_output: A PUT request with the full application payload including the existing primary matched by member_id and a new dependent without member_id.
assertions: Uses PUT method to /api/v1/applications/HSA000738818, not PATCH
Sends the FULL application payload, not just changed fields
Includes member_id: '12345' on the primary applicant object for matching
New spouse dependent OMITS the member_id field so HealthSherpa creates it
Dependent has relationship: 'spouse' set
Uses gender for the dependent, not sex
Includes signatures.signature_date and applicants.primary.signature in the full payload

evals.prompt: Build a webhook handler for HealthSherpa policy status updates. It should process all status transitions, verify authenticity, and be safe to receive duplicate deliveries.
evals.expected_output: A webhook endpoint that verifies the signature, responds quickly, processes async, and handles each status with appropriate business logic.
assertions: Verifies X-Webhook-Signature header before processing the payload
Returns a 2xx response immediately, before performing business logic
Processes the event asynchronously (queue, background job, or similar)
Deduplicates using application_id + policy_status + timestamp
Handles effectuated status to trigger HRA reimbursement start or coverage activation
Handles cancelled status to stop future reimbursements
Handles terminated status to reconcile retroactive changes, may require clawback
Handles pending_effectuation status to record submission, does not treat as active coverage
Handles submission_failed status to alert or retry
Does not assume events arrive in order

evals.prompt: After quoting plans, some have api_enrollment: true and others only have deeplink_enrollment: true. Write a function that routes enrollment to the correct path. For API-enrollable plans, create an application via EnrollConnect. For deeplink-only plans, redirect the user through the HealthSherpa deeplink. Handle plans that are not enrollable. Also build the UI for selecting and enrolling in a plan.
evals.expected_output: Code that checks both enrollment flags on the plan and routes accordingly, with proper 302 handling for deeplinks. UI shows a single 'Enroll' button, not backend flag labels.
assertions: Checks api_enrollment and deeplink_enrollment flags to determine routing
Routes api_enrollment: true plans to POST /api/v1/applications on the ICHRA proxy base URL
Routes deeplink-only plans to POST /public/ichra/off_ex on the HealthSherpa base URL (different host)
Handles 302 redirect for deeplinks — captures Location header, does NOT follow server-side
Uses redirect: 'manual' (or equivalent) on the deeplink fetch call
Includes _agent_id in the deeplink payload (it is required for deeplinks)
Throws an error or returns a clear message when both flags are false
Does not attempt EnrollConnect for a plan with api_enrollment: false
Does NOT expose 'API Enrollment' or 'Deeplink Enrollment' labels to end users — these are internal routing flags
Shows a single 'Enroll' or 'Select Plan' button — the routing decision happens in the backend, not the UI
Enrollment form/page requires a plan_hios_id — does not allow plan-less enrollment

evals.prompt: Write a polling function that monitors an application after submission until it reaches a final status. It should use appropriate intervals and handle the case where the application stays in pending_effectuation for an extended period.
evals.expected_output: A polling function with escalating intervals that tracks status transitions and alerts on stale applications.
assertions: Calls GET /api/v1/applications/:id to check status
Sends x-api-key header on every request
Polls more frequently right after submission (every ~5 minutes for the first hour)
Reduces frequency for awaiting effectuation (~30 minutes)
Drops to steady-state interval (~4 hours) or switches to webhook-driven
Treats pending_effectuation as an expected intermediate state, not an error
Handles submission_failed status as an error requiring attention (alert or retry)
Flags or alerts if pending_effectuation persists beyond 48 hours
Terminates polling on final states: effectuated, cancelled, terminated
Reads policy_status from the response, not a separate field

evals.prompt: A user provides only a zip code. Before quoting, I need to resolve it to a FIPS code because some zip codes span multiple counties. Write a county selector that calls the CMS Marketplace API to look up counties, and if there are multiple, prompts the user to select one before proceeding to quote.
evals.expected_output: Code that calls the CMS county lookup API, handles single vs multiple counties, and passes the selected FIPS code to the quoting function.
assertions: Calls GET https://marketplace-int.api.healthcare.gov/api/v1/counties/by/zip/{zipcode} (the public CMS API, not a HealthSherpa endpoint)
Does NOT require an API key for the CMS call
Handles the single-county case by auto-selecting
Handles the multi-county case by presenting options to the user
Extracts the fips field from the selected county object
Passes the fips value as fip_code in the quoting request (note the field name difference)
Handles the zero-county case (invalid or unsupported zip) with a clear error

evals.prompt: Build a complete ICHRA enrollment dashboard page that shows all applications for my platform. It should support filtering by status, searching by employee name, pagination, and show key details for each application. Include a way to detect applications that need attention (stuck in pending, failed submissions, docs needed).
evals.expected_output: A dashboard component that calls GET /applications with appropriate filters, paginates, and highlights applications needing attention.
assertions: Calls GET /api/v1/applications with query parameters for filtering
Uses policy_status filter parameter for status filtering
Uses limit and offset parameters for pagination
Uses updated_since parameter for incremental sync
Uses application_id from each list item to link to the detail view
Uses primary_applicant.first_name/last_name for display — NOT applicants.primary (list uses different structure than detail)
Highlights applications with submission_failed status
Highlights applications with sep_docs_required status
Highlights applications stuck in pending_effectuation for more than 48 hours
Reads pagination.total_count (not pagination.total) from the list response

evals.prompt: Write an API client wrapper for the HealthSherpa EnrollConnect API that handles response normalization. The create/get response nests data under an 'application' key while metadata is at the top level. The plan lookup nests under a 'plan' key. Handle both correctly.
evals.expected_output: An API client with proper response normalization that flattens nested responses.
assertions: Flattens the create/get response by merging response.application into the top level alongside application_id, errors, next_actions, payment_instructions
Does NOT assume application_id is inside response.application — it's only at the top level
Unwraps plan lookup responses from response.plan
Reads list items using primary_applicant.first_name/last_name — NOT applicants.primary
Uses application_id from list items to link to detail view
Reads pagination.total_count from list responses — NOT pagination.total

evals.prompt: An ICHRA platform partner has a member whose email address was entered incorrectly during enrollment. The application has already been submitted and effectuated. Write the code to correct the email via the EnrollConnect API. The application ID is HSA000739499. Show the full workflow including checking eligibility, sending the update, and resubmitting.
evals.expected_output: Code that checks supports_changes, sends a PUT with the corrected email while preserving identity fields, and resubmits.
assertions: GETs the application first and checks supports_changes is true before attempting changes
Sends a PUT to /api/v1/applications/:id with the full payload including all identity fields (first_name, last_name, date_of_birth) preserved from the original
Does NOT omit identity fields from the PUT — omitted fields are treated as null
Calls POST /api/v1/applications/:id/submit after the PUT to resubmit changes to the carrier
Handles the case where supports_changes is false by informing the user to contact the carrier directly
Checks can_report_change to confirm demographic changes are allowed

evals.prompt: A carrier has knockout questions that make certain applicants ineligible. Write error handling code for the HealthSherpa EnrollConnect API that properly handles the ineligible_for_enrollment error when creating or updating an application. Show how to detect it, explain what it means, and show the correct user-facing response.
evals.expected_output: Error handling that recognizes ineligible_for_enrollment as a deterministic carrier rejection and presents an appropriate message.
assertions: Parses the errors array from the API response
Identifies the ineligible_for_enrollment error code
Treats it as a deterministic (non-retryable) error — does NOT retry the same request
Presents a user-friendly message indicating the applicant is not eligible for this plan with this carrier
Suggests selecting a different plan or carrier as the resolution path

evals.prompt: Write code that fetches a HealthSherpa EnrollConnect application and displays a human-readable change history from the events timeline. The application ID is HSA000739499. Show how to parse different event types and handle redacted fields.
evals.expected_output: Code that fetches the application, iterates the events array, and formats each event type into readable output.
assertions: GETs /api/v1/applications/:id (with include_events=true or default)
Iterates the events array from the response
Handles submitted events by displaying the occurred_at timestamp and response_code
Handles changed events by displaying target (application or applicant), member_id for applicant changes, and the changes array with field/from/to
Handles policy_status_updated events by displaying old_status and new_status
Recognizes that sensitive fields in changes may be redacted (from/to shown as null or omitted)
